page: Google Cloud Platform c4-highmem-96 is a memory-optimized server instance powered by an Intel Xeon 8581C processor. It features 96 virtual CPUs mapped to 48 physical cores with hyperthreading enabled, running at a base clock speed of 2.3 GHz. The instance is configured with 744 GB of system memory, providing approximately 7.75 GB of RAM per vCPU. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, relying entirely on network-attached storage for persistent disk operations.

Benchmark data indicates strong multi-core processing and memory capabilities. The instance achieved a multi-core stress-ng CPU score of 82,697.57 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 21,073.0. Memory bandwidth is notable, with read-only large block transfers reaching 288.74 GB/sec and write large block transfers at 124.06 GB/sec. The CPU architecture supports advanced vector and matrix extensions, including AVX-512 and AMX, which contributes to strong performance in specialized tasks such as a Passmark CPU encryption score of 87,292.07 and medium-model (7B) LLM prompt processing at 297.98 tokens/sec.

From a resource density perspective, the c4-highmem-96 offers high memory allocation per core without the added cost of bundled local NVMe storage or GPU accelerators. This makes it a qualitatively cost-efficient option for workloads that require large memory footprints and high CPU thread counts but do not need local disk persistence or hardware-accelerated graphics. However, the lack of local storage means all storage operations must rely on network-attached volumes, which may introduce latency tradeoffs compared to local NVMe-equipped instances.

This server configuration is designed for memory-intensive applications, large-scale database operations, software compilation, and text or image processing pipelines. It is also capable of handling CPU-based large language model inference, particularly for prompt processing where its matrix extensions provide a significant speedup.
description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highmem-96 is an x86_64 server instance featuring 96 vCPUs and 744 GB of RAM, powered by an Intel Xeon 8581C processor running at 2.3 GHz. With 48 physical cores and hyperthreading, it delivers strong multi-core performance, as evidenced by a Geekbench multi-core score of 21,073.0 and a stress-ng multi-core CPU score of 82,697.57. The architecture includes AVX-512 and AMX extensions, enabling efficient cryptographic and matrix operations, including medium-model LLM prompt processing at 297.98 tokens/sec. Lacking local storage and dedicated GPUs, this instance optimizes cost efficiency for memory-heavy workloads by avoiding bundled hardware overhead. It is suited for large-scale database operations, software compilation, memory-intensive enterprise applications, and CPU-based machine learning inference.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highmem-96 features 96 vCPUs, 744 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon 8581C processor, delivering strong multi-core performance and high memory bandwidth for enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highmem-96 is a memory-optimized instance with 96 vCPUs, 744 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon CPU, built for database workloads.
tagline: A high-memory x86_64 server featuring ninety-six virtual CPUs and advanced matrix extensions for memory-intensive enterprise workloads and database operations.
bullet_points: 96 vCPUs and 744 GB RAM powered by an Intel Xeon 8581C processor
Advanced vector and matrix extensions (AVX-512 and AMX) for accelerated computing
Strong multi-core performance with a Geekbench multi-core score of 21,073.0
No local storage or GPU hardware, optimizing cost for pure memory and compute needs
Suited for large databases, software compilation, and CPU-based LLM inference
